commit.message:
fix: write Linux perf reports to /output so they survive the container

PERF_REPORT_DIR=/project/tests was wrong: in cibuildwheel's manylinux
container /project is mounted read-only for the test step, so conftest.py
silently failed to write the perf report (OSError caught).

For non-Linux platforms cibuildwheel runs natively and {package} expands
to the workspace source dir, so conftest __file__ resolves to the source
tests/ — this is why macOS/Windows reports always worked.

Fix:
- Set PERF_REPORT_DIR=/output in both Linux package jobs; /output is
  bind-mounted read-write and docker-cp'd back to wheelhouse/ after the run
- Update artifact upload paths to wheelhouse/perf_report.* accordingly

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix climber transpiler bugs: Mul handler + stelem self-referencing arrays (#156)

* Update climber sample GIF to reflect PR #155 transpiler fixes

The compound expression handling fixes from PR #155 (EmitOamSprDecsp4,
dup cascade, oam_spr arg scanning) now produce correct 6502 code for
the climber sample. The stale NuGet package was preventing the sample
from picking up these fixes.

The game now correctly renders:
- 4+ actors with proper metasprite tiles
- Score display with computed tile indices (0x30 + (score >> 4))
- OAM offset properly aligned (mod4=0), preventing oam_hide_rest hang
- 18 visible sprites at runtime (4 metasprites + 2 score digits)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Mul handler: detect ldloc-loaded locals for runtime multiply

The Mul handler only checked _runtimeValueInA for ASL shift optimization,
but WriteLdloc doesn't set that flag. This caused ldloc;ldc;mul patterns
(e.g. gap * 16) to:
1. Emit orphaned JSR pusha calls that leaked C stack bytes
2. Skip the ASL shift multiplication entirely

Added _lastLoadedLocalIndex detection to the Mul handler (matching the
pattern used by AND, DIV, SHR, SHL, and other operators). When a local
variable is in A, the handler now removes the unnecessary pusha+LDA
instructions emitted by WriteLdc and emits proper ASL shifts.

This fixes the climber sample crash: 4 orphaned pusha calls per frame
leaked 4 bytes/frame on the C software stack, eventually overwriting
FamiTone2 BSS at \ and causing an infinite loop in the music
engine's NMI handler.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update climber screenshots with fixed rendering

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix stelem self-referencing array update pattern

HandleStelemI1 now correctly handles arr[i] = arr[i] ± N patterns
(e.g., actor_x[pi] = (byte)(actor_x[pi] - 1)). Previously, these
fell through to the constant-store fallback, storing a literal value
instead of modifying the existing array element.

The fix adds a new code path that detects when sourceArray1Idx matches
targetArrayLocalIdx and emits LDX idx; LDA arr,X; SEC/CLC; SBC/ADC N;
STA arr,X — proper read-modify-write indexed addressing.

This fixes player movement (LEFT/RIGHT) and independent enemy movement
in the climber sample.

Also fixed stale NuGet packaging: dotnet pack requires -c Debug to use
the Debug configuration DLL (defaults to Release otherwise).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update climber GIF with working movement

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update climber GIF using ANESE for consistent sizing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Mul handler: remove only LDA #const, not LDA \

WriteLdloc emits LDA Absolute which sets LastLDA=false, so WriteLdc
only emits LDA #constant (no pusha). RemoveLastInstructions(2) was
incorrectly removing the local load as well. Changed to remove 1.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add regression test for Mul handler RemoveLastInstructions bug

The test verifies that ldloc;ldc;mul preserves the LDA  instruction.
With RemoveLastInstructions(2), the local load was incorrectly removed,
leaving ASL shifts operating on a stale accumulator value from pal_col.
The test asserts that LDA Absolute (AD) appears immediately before the
16-bit shift setup (LDX #0, STX , ASL A, ROL ).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add dedicated avatar and cover image endpoints (#69)

## Summary

Adds lightweight dedicated endpoints for fetching user avatars and event
covers, enabling lazy-loading on the frontend.

### New endpoints
- `GET /profile/{user_id}/avatar` → `{"avatar": "base64..." | null}`
- `GET /events/{event_id}/cover` → `{"cover": "base64..." | null}`

### Why
List endpoints were refactored to exclude `avatar`/`cover` fields for
performance. These new per-item image endpoints let the frontend
lazy-load images after the page renders, keeping list payloads small.

### Changes
- `app/schemas/profile.py` — new `AvatarResponse` schema
- `app/schemas/event.py` — new `CoverResponse` schema
- `app/api/routes/profile/get_other_profile.py` — new `GET
/{user_id}/avatar` route
- `app/api/routes/events/get_cover.py` — new `GET /{event_id}/cover`
route (registered before `/{event_id}` for correct precedence)

### Related
Frontend PR: iu-alumni/iu-alumni-frontend feat/lazy-image-loading

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: plugin agents use folder/agent ID format for CLI --agent flag (#516)

* fix: plugin agents use folder/agent ID format for CLI --agent flag

Plugin agents in installed-plugins/ with agency.json manifests now get
IDs prefixed with the plugin folder name (e.g., dev-team/dev-team instead
of just dev-team). This matches the Copilot CLI's expected --agent format.

Also discovers ALL agents from a plugin's agents/ directory, not just
the entry-point agent. The PluginManifest now includes allAgentPaths.

Closes #515

install-k3s-server: disable built-in servicelb (conflicts with MetalLB)

prevents MetalLB from setting up iptables DNAT rules for LoadBalancer IPs.

commit.message:
chore: bump to v4.0.0-alpha.13

Consolidates all daemon reliability work (P0-P3):
- P0: idle timeout, request IDs, session cleanup, client timeouts
- P1: health tracking, uptime, atomic state, file lock
- P2: session_create, daemon_config, daemon_logs + typed ErrorCode
- P3: dynamic runtime dir, stale PID cleanup, socket perms, per-cmd timeouts

Full E2E verified: 18/18 daemon tests pass.

commit.message:
chore: expand site description in VitePress config

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Implement automatic experiment loop resumption

The loop now reads run-metadata.json on startup and resumes from the
last completed experiment instead of always starting at 1.

State restored on resume:
- Experiment counter (startExperiment = last + 1)
- StaleCount and ConsecutiveFailures counters (now persisted per
  experiment in metadata for this purpose)
- Best P95 / best experiment / success count
- Stacked-diffs branch state: currentBranch, lastSuccessfulBranch,
  branchChain, prChain, failedExperiments

Semantics:
- -MaxExperiments N means 'run N more' (not absolute cap)
- Resumes from the last attempted branch (not last successful),
  preserving learnings from failed experiments
- Get-PerformanceBaseline.ps1 resets Experiments to empty, which
  naturally triggers a fresh start from experiment 1

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Generate static blog post redirect pages at build time

DropInBlog API and generates static HTML pages at blog/<slug>/index.htm.

hash-based tab navigation is deterministic.

fix: remove Token validation from FluentValidation rules and unify SignalR connection tracking

Token is now extracted from Authorization header in controllers, not from
request body. Validators requiring Token.NotEmpty() blocked all live session
requests (chat, cinema, soundboard, featured image, end/leave).

Also unified participant connection tracking: LiveSessionHub now uses
RealtimeService<LiveSessionHub> static methods instead of a separate
dictionary, fixing whisper/direct message delivery.

fix(daemon): P3 hardening — dynamic runtime dir, stale PID, socket perms

- Runtime directory: $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/onecrawl > $TMPDIR/onecrawl > /tmp
  with 0o700 permissions (was: hardcoded /tmp/)
- Socket file gets 0o600 permissions after bind (owner-only access)
- Stale PID detection: is_daemon_running() validates PID liveness and
  auto-cleans dead PID/socket files (was: stale files blocked startup)
- Legacy /tmp file cleanup on daemon start for migration from older versions
- All daemon file paths now generated dynamically via protocol functions

commit.message:
feat: YAML Event Schema Parser (#4)

* feat(schema): implement YAML event schema parser with 18 validation rules (#4)

Implements GitHub Issue #4: [All.Schema] YAML Event Schema Parser.

Schema Models:
- EventDefinition, FieldDefinition, EnumDefinition, MetricDefinition
- SchemaDocument, SchemaHeader with full YAML grammar support
- FieldType enum with 12 types (string, int, long, double, bool, etc.)
- Sensitivity enum (public, internal, pii, credential)
- Severity enum (TRACE, DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR, FATAL)
- MetricType enum (counter, histogram, gauge)

YAML Parser (SchemaParser):
- Safe YAML loading via YamlDotNet RepresentationModel
- Resource limits: 1MB file size, max nesting depth 20
- Full grammar parsing: schema header, imports, fields, enums, events
- Supports field attributes: type, ref, sensitivity, maxLength, index
- Supports metrics with buckets and labels

Schema Validation (SchemaValidator):
- ALL_SCHEMA_001: Unique event names
- ALL_SCHEMA_002: Valid severity levels
- ALL_SCHEMA_003: Message template placeholder matching
- ALL_SCHEMA_004: Ref resolution (fields and enums)
- ALL_SCHEMA_005: Type validity
- ALL_SCHEMA_006: Event name format (lowercase dot-namespaced)
- ALL_SCHEMA_007: Required field completeness
- ALL_SCHEMA_008: Metric type validity
- ALL_SCHEMA_009: Enum non-empty
- ALL_SCHEMA_010: Semver version
- ALL_SCHEMA_011: Reserved 'all.' prefix
- ALL_SCHEMA_012: Unique numeric IDs
- ALL_SCHEMA_013: Valid meter name
- ALL_SCHEMA_014: Sensitivity validity
- ALL_SCHEMA_015: maxLength validity
- ALL_SCHEMA_016: File size limit (1MB)
- ALL_SCHEMA_017: Event count limit (500)
- ALL_SCHEMA_018: Field count limit (50 per event)

Schema Merging (SchemaMerger):
- Merges multiple SchemaDocument instances
- Resolves imports with circular dependency detection
- Cross-document validation (duplicate names/IDs across files)

Tests: 106 tests, all passing (SchemaParserTests, SchemaValidatorTests,
SchemaMergerTests, SchemaEdgeCaseTests)

* fix(schema): add YAML alias/anchor rejection and edge case tests

Security fixes:
- Add pre-parse scan for YAML anchors (&) and aliases (*) to prevent
  YAML bomb attacks (exponential memory expansion). Uses YamlDotNet
  Parser event stream to detect AnchorAlias and NodeEvent with anchors
  before loading the representation model. (HIGH severity QA finding)
- Add error code ALL_SCHEMA_019 (YamlAliasRejected) for alias rejection

Validation improvements:
- Add ALL_SCHEMA_020 (DuplicateEnumValue) validation rule — enums with
  duplicate values now produce an error during schema validation
- Duplicate detection uses HashSet for O(n) performance

Test coverage:
- Parse_YamlWithAliasAndAnchor_RejectsOrIgnores
- Parse_YamlWithAnchorOnly_Rejects
- Parse_YamlBomb_Rejects
- Parse_ValidYamlWithoutAliases_Succeeds (regression guard)
- Parse_YamlWithTagDirective_RejectsOrIgnores
- Parse_YamlWithNodeTag_IgnoresTag
- Validate_DuplicateEnumValues_ReturnsError
- Validate_UniqueEnumValues_NoError
- Parse_ArrayFieldType_ParsesCorrectly (string[], int[])
- Parse_ArrayFieldTypeShorthand_ParsesCorrectly
- Parse_MapFieldType_ParsesCorrectly
- Parse_MapFieldTypeShorthand_ParsesCorrectly

All 118 tests pass.

* fix: use InvariantCulture for double.Parse in bucket parsing

Prevents FormatException on non-US locales where ',' is decimal separator.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  lucro previsto/realizado, histórico de custos, previsão de estoque

- Parsers específicos: AliExpress, Alibaba e genérico de fallback

  alertas de estoque e ranking de ROI

  (Dashboard, Produtos, Fornecedores, Importações, Vendas, Recibos)

- README completo em pt-BR com instruções de setup

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Use PSReadLine internal ForceRender() instead of InvokePrompt()

InvokePrompt() redraws the entire prompt, which corrupts oh-my-posh
Powerline characters and causes a slow jarring flash.

ForceRender() is PSReadLine's internal method that forces a full
non-differential render of just the input buffer without touching the
prompt. Accessed via reflection with the singleton/method cached at
install time. Falls back to default Replace() rendering if the
internal API is unavailable.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Update api/main.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

consensus: Fix minority fork sync deadlock

Three interconnected issues prevented nodes on minority forks from
recovering:

1. Block reward validation errors (incorrect block_reward, pool theft)
   were not recognized as fork signals. These errors occur when the
   local UTXO set diverges from the consensus chain, producing different
   fee calculations. Now triggers fork resolution instead of silently
   skipping blocks.

2. Non-consensus peer blocking threshold was too high (>20 blocks).
   Nodes 18-19 blocks behind were stuck in limbo - too far for direct
   sync, too close for the deadlock bypass. Lowered to >10 blocks.

3. sync_from_specific_peer failed with 'No chain tip data' at startup
   when peers hadn't sent ChainTipResponse yet. Now falls back to
   requesting a small batch of blocks when consensus exists but chain
   tip is unknown.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix chat session save/resume/message_count

- Fix resume button in Sessions panel: now actually loads session into Chat
  (was only switching tabs without loading messages)
- Add auto-save after every completed AI response (no more losing chats)
- Fix stale closure bug: use currentSessionIdRef so auto-saves update same
  session instead of creating duplicates on every response
- Fix message_count always showing 0 in Sessions list (now counts user messages)
- Sync ref on all session state changes: load, delete, new/clear chat
